notes.text: Opening Image: the world feels like ash and aftermath—war continues, but hope is something you have to earn again.
Theme stated: liberation and sacrifice are inseparable; refusing sacrifice keeps the cycle alive.
Set-Up: Neo and the team regroup, planning the next phase of resistance with urgency and exhaustion.
He is burdened by what he knows—his courage becomes the decision to keep moving anyway.
Catalyst: the city is attacked; the plan stops being hypothetical and becomes a fight for survival.
She refuses to retreat—her bravery becomes the emotional engine for the crew.
Debate: the team argues between speed and safety, between doing 'enough' and doing 'right'.
Break into Two: the resistance commits to a full push, choosing risk over controlled timing.
Her B-story thread keeps the story grounded—logistics, leadership, and ethics under fire.
Fun and Games (in darker form): brief momentum before the next wave arrives—small wins that keep morale from collapsing.
Midpoint: the conflict turns personal; a betrayal or irreversible step changes what victory would even mean.
He tightens the net—his threat becomes less about destruction and more about controlling outcomes.
Bad Guys Close In: the resistance is compressed, forced into hard choices with no clean escape route.
All Is Lost: the mission fails in the most painful way, leaving the team with only one remaining path forward.
His role becomes a final lesson—steady guidance when the world goes quiet.
Dark Night of the Soul: grief and responsibility converge; the hero confronts what it means to continue.
Neo is captured / cornered (choose your exact beat): the story forces helplessness before the breakthrough.
Break into Three: the hero chooses sacrifice not as defeat, but as a strategy for transformation.
Finale: multiple threads collide; pay off the emotional reversal with visible action and hard consequence.
Optional structure note: pick the single most 'rewriting the rules' moment and expand it in your draft.
Final Image: a fragile new dawn—proof that change is possible even after the cost is paid.
Resolution theme: liberation becomes responsibility, not just survival.
